prompt: A short, crisp, tight metallic-crystal clunk impact sound of a low-poly sci-fantasy defense tower being placed onto a build spot. Combination of light metal and crystal materials colliding, quick snappy decay, no low bass boom, satisfying but not deep.
seed_note: ElevenLabs SFX v2はseed固定不可のため共通語彙プロンプトで4変種生成しゲーム内文脈でベスト選別
selection_reason: 最高クレストファクター（peak/RMS比、最大6.5）＝立ち上がりが鋭く減衰が速い『軽く歯切れよい』設置音の要件に最も合致。他候補(v1,v2,v4)はクレスト比が低く、より鈍い/持続的な音色だった。
duration_deviation_note: API duration_seconds下限0.5s + 生成後の無音部分トリム（実音声部のみ抽出）により、design/assets.md記載の想定尺と実測尺が異なる（SFX-02のみ実測がspecより長い）。トリムはsilencedetect(-45dB)で確認した本物の無音パディングのみを除去しており、減衰テールを削っていない。
loudness_method: peak-normalize(target -1.5dBTP) — LUFS未測定: 0.22s は EBU R128 統合ラウドネスのゲーティング最小長を下回り ffmpeg loudnorm が input_i=-inf を返す既知の技術的制約のため
cost_estimate_basis: ElevenLabs Starter プラン公式価格 $5/mo=30,000クレジット換算（$0.0001667/credit）。API応答は character-cost ヘッダのクレジット数のみでUSD直接表記なし。
license_note: ElevenLabs Starter($6/mo)以上 = 商用利用可（state/asset-routing.json checks.elevenlabs.plan_tier=starter, commercial_ok=true を preflight で実測済み）

pipeline: elevenlabs music_v2 (composition_plan, 2 sections x 19200ms) -> ffmpeg loudnorm(-16 LUFS linear 2-pass) -> bar-boundary loop crossfade edit (last 300ms tail acrossfade-blended into first 300ms head, body 0-38.1s + blended 300ms, total duration unchanged 38.4s) -> 2x self-concat click/RMS-step scan (pass) -> re-scan after libvorbis encode (pass) -> OGG Vorbis 145kbps
prompt: composition_plan positive_global_styles: light fantasy orchestral video game background music, medium tempo 100 BPM, D major, bright and uplifting, pizzicato strings, woodwind lead (flute, clarinet), light brass accents, glockenspiel and triangle sparkle, instrumental only, written as an endlessly repeating game loop cue with a hard loop point not a song with an ending, constant instrumentation density and constant loudness maintained from the first beat to the very last beat. negative_global_styles: dark, heavy, vocals, lyrics, distorted, aggressive, lo-fi, ambient drone, fade out, fade in, ritardando, rallentando, diminuendo, coda, outro, ending sting, final cadence, resolution held with a long decaying tail, getting quieter near the end, song structure with an intro and outro. SectionA(19200ms): woodwind flute/clarinet main melody, pizzicato strings accompaniment, lighter texture than section B but full tempo/volume not a fade-in intro. SectionB(19200ms): light brass joins, restrained timpani/light percussion, glockenspiel/triangle sparkle, fuller energy sustained through the very last beat, cuts directly to loop with no ritardando/taper/held chord/cymbal swell. lines: [] (force instrumental via empty lyric lines; composition_plan API rejects force_instrumental param directly "force_instrumental can only be used with prompt" — 422 confirmed).
seed_note: Eleven Music v1/music API has no seed parameter (confirmed: not accepted in composition_plan or prompt mode during this generation). Provenance identifier used instead: song-id response header. 3 generation attempts made (see generation_attempts); song-id of ACCEPTED attempt = AjlJ6ygaTHlbBz111th7.

generation_attempts.reason: Despite prompt/composition_plan explicitly requesting no fade in/out and a hard loop point, the model appended a ~1.1-2s exponential fade-to-silence at the tail (RMS decayed from -13dB at 34.2s to -82dB by 38.2s, silencedetect -45dB triggered 37.27-38.4s). Would have produced an audible silence-to-full-volume jump at the loop point. Discarded before any post-processing.

Stronger anti-fade wording in negative_global_styles/negative_local_styles caused the model to overcorrect: produced 6.36s of near-silence (-89dB) in the last third of the track (silencedetect -45dB triggered 32.04-38.4s) — an even larger dead-air region, unusable for looping. Discarded before any post-processing.

Reframed composition_plan as a repeating game-loop cue (not a song with an ending) and added explicit "constant loudness to the very last beat" language. Result has no silence anywhere (silencedetect -45dB: zero hits across full 38.4s) and only a normal ~100-200ms note-release decay at the tail, comparable in magnitude to natural note attacks/decays throughout the piece. RMS level at the tail region (-27 to -34dB in the last ~2s) closely matches the RMS level at the head (-33 to -37dB in the first ~0.5s), which is why the crossfade edit produced a seam with no anomalous click or level step (see loop_validation).

cost_estimate_basis: assets-config.md BGM pricing rate /bin/zsh.15/min (no character-cost/credit header returned by /v1/music responses for any of the 3 attempts, unlike /v1/sound-generation which returns one for SFX). 38.4s = 0.64min -> /bin/zsh.096 for the accepted generation; 3 attempts total (all same 38.4s length) = /bin/zsh.288. Only the accepted attempt (song-id AjlJ6ygaTHlbBz111th7) cost is carried in cost_usd; the other 2 discarded attempts cost is disclosed here per the exploratory-cost convention already used for MDL-01/02 discarded concept images.
loop_edit_method: Bar-boundary crossfade: 300ms tail [38.1-38.4s] acrossfade-blended (triangular curve) with 300ms head [0-0.3s], replacing the original last 300ms; total duration unchanged (38.1s untouched body + 300ms blended segment = 38.4s).

loop_validation.method: 2x self-concatenation + sample-delta click scan + 50ms-window RMS-step scan, run twice: once on the pre-encode loudnorm+crossfade WAV, once on the final encoded OGG (post-lossy-compression) to confirm the seam survives encoding.

click_scan_pre_encode.verdict: pass (seam delta well below typical in-track transient deltas; no click)

rms_step_scan_pre_encode.verdict: pass (seam step at/below median natural fluctuation; no audible level discontinuity)

loop_validation.silencedetect_full_track_neg45dB: zero hits (no dead air anywhere in the final track)

loudness_method: ffmpeg loudnorm 2-pass linear (measured then applied): I=-16 LUFS target, TP=-1.5 dBTP, LRA=11
force_instrumental: requested via empty lyric lines ([]) in every composition_plan section, since the force_instrumental boolean param is rejected (422) when composition_plan is used instead of prompt. Forms built from simple primitives -- cones, hexagonal prisms, domes, blocky stubby limbs -- chosen so silhouettes stay readable at small on-screen size. Plain, uncluttered ground/backdrop when rendering single-subject concept turnarounds -- no competing background detail, no crowd of secondary objects, no text, no watermark, no UI overlay. Two-icon flat silhouette badge sprite sheet on a transparent background, arranged side by side with clear spacing, used as a wave-warning HUD indicator: (1) Marauder badge -- a small bipedal creature silhouette standing upright on two legs, body color hex #973FA5 with accent color hex #C71A23; (2) Warbeast badge -- a larger quadruped (four-legged) creature silhouette, same color palette (body hex #973FA5, accent hex #C71A23), overall visual bulk approximately 1.7x larger than the Marauder badge. ONLY the horns, the glowing eyes, and the very tips of the claws must be a contrasting deep red color hex #C71A23 -- these red parts are small, thin accent shapes (roughly 10-15% of the area total) sitting ON TOP OF the purple body, never the other way around. Do NOT make the main body/torso/limbs red -- red is reserved exclusively for horns, eyes, and claw-tips. If in doubt, render more of the creature in purple and less in red. The two badges must be distinguishable by bipedal-vs-quadruped silhouette shape alone, clearly readable even after downscaling to a 256px thumbnail size.
